questions.question: A ball is dropped from a height. As it falls toward the ground, what is the main energy transformation?
questions.explanation: As the ball falls, it loses height (PE decreases) and gains speed (KE increases). PE is converted to KE.

questions.question: A ball is thrown straight upward. As it rises and slows down, what is the main energy transformation?
options: Heat energy → Kinetic energy → Gravitational potential energy
Kinetic energy → Electrical energy → Gravitational potential energy
questions.explanation: As the ball rises, it loses speed (KE decreases) and gains height (PE increases). KE is converted to PE.

questions.question: A moving cart hits a spring and compresses it. What is the main energy transformation as the spring compresses?
questions.explanation: The cart's motion (KE) is transferred to the spring, which stores it as elastic (strain) potential energy.

questions.question: A stretched bowstring is released, launching an arrow forward. What is the main energy transformation?
questions.explanation: The stored elastic potential energy in the stretched bowstring is converted into kinetic energy of the arrow.

questions.question: In a hydroelectric dam, falling water spins a turbine connected to a generator. What is the main energy transformation in the generator?
questions.explanation: The turbine provides mechanical (kinetic) energy to the generator, which converts it into electrical energy.

questions.question: A ceiling fan is switched on. What is the main energy transformation?
questions.explanation: The fan motor converts electrical energy from the mains into mechanical (kinetic) energy of the rotating blades.

questions.question: A carpenter rubs two sticks together to start a fire. What is the main energy transformation?
questions.explanation: The mechanical energy of rubbing (friction) is converted into heat energy, which ignites the kindling.

questions.question: An incandescent light bulb is turned on. Which energy transformations occur? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The bulb converts electrical energy into both light energy (useful) and heat energy (wasted). This is why incandescent bulbs are inefficient.

questions.question: A car engine burns fuel to move the vehicle. What is the main energy transformation?
options: Mechanical energy → Chemical energy + Heat energy
Electrical energy + Chemical energy → Mechanical energy
Chemical energy → Mechanical energy + Heat energy
Electrical energy → Chemical energy + Mechanical energy
questions.explanation: The engine burns fuel (chemical energy) and converts it into mechanical energy to move the car, with some energy lost as heat.

questions.question: A solar panel on a rooftop powers a home. What is the main energy transformation?
questions.explanation: Solar panels convert light (solar) energy from the Sun directly into electrical energy.

questions.question: A spacecraft re-enters Earth's atmosphere and its surface becomes extremely hot. What is the main energy transformation?
questions.explanation: The spacecraft's high-speed motion (KE) is converted into heat energy due to friction with the atmosphere.

questions.question: A pendulum swings from its highest point to its lowest point and back up. Which energy transformations occur during one complete swing?
options: Strain energy → KE (downward), then KE → Strain energy (upward)
Strain energy → KE (upward), then KE → Strain energy (downward)
questions.explanation: On the downswing, gravitational PE converts to KE. On the upswing, KE converts back to gravitational PE.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered energy transformations across all systems!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review mechanical-to-electrical and electrical-to-mechanical transformations.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on identifying the initial energy form and what it converts into.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: PE → KE, KE → PE, and common transformations in everyday devices.
